description: Multi-phase plans currently in flight. Each phase references roadmap item titles; the activePhase section breaks the current phase into concrete tasks with status. Survives conversation compactions — a fresh session can pick up by reading this file.

phases.title: Plugin runtime — manifest, SDK, federation hooks
roadmapItems: Plugin runtime — manifest format, loader, SDK, federation hooks
phases.notes: Shipped. PluginManifest / definePlugin / server + client loaders / auto-mounted federation routes / hello-world reference plugin.

phases.title: Polls & Quizzes plugin — first SDK consumer
roadmapItems: Polls & Quizzes — first plugin (validates simple SDK case)
phases.notes: Shipped end-to-end: schema migrations, REST routes, federation handler, real-time sync via babelr:ws custom event, translation wired manually (to be automated in Phase 3), list-detail view via the new ListDetailView primitive, sidebar-slot button opens the view.

phases.title: SDK gap-closure based on polls implementation
roadmapItems: Translation as default for plugin content — zero-config primitive
phases.notes: All four in-scope tasks shipped: component ergonomics (ComponentType + createElement), translation-by-default (<T>), WS helper (onWsMessage), and documented the server-safe/client-only file-split convention. Plugin authors now have one-liners for the friction points polls exposed. Phase 4 (project management) starts on a smoother SDK. Deferred items persisted to roadmap: 'Plugin SDK — stable re-exports of @babelr/client utilities' (P2, ships with Phase 5), 'Plugin DB abstraction — helper over drizzle for plugin tables' (P3, revisit after PM), 'Plugin test harness — standard pattern for plugin unit + integration tests' (P2, builds alongside PM), and cross-Tower voting is already covered by 'Validate polls federation across two-Tower staging' (P2).

phases.title: Project management plugin — complex SDK consumer
roadmapItems: Project management — second plugin (validates complex SDK case)
phases.notes: Multiple embed kinds, complex schema, plugin-provided view (kanban), AS2 custom objects for federation, drag-and-drop UI. Validates SDK at scale after Phase 3 closes known gaps.

phases.title: Plugin distribution — npm packaging, admin install, manifest freeze
roadmapItems: Plugin distribution — npm packaging, admin install flow, manifest freeze
phases.notes: After polls + PM prove the contract, freeze the manifest and ship public-facing distribution.

activePhase.title: Project management — complex SDK consumer
activePhase.goal: Build PM as the second first-party plugin, validating the SDK at complex scale. Multiple embed kinds, plugin-provided view, drag-and-drop UI, AS2 federation of custom objects, comment threads via useChat. Prove the Phase 3 primitives (<T>, onWsMessage, ComponentType registries, split-file pattern) scale before Phase 5 freezes the manifest.
activePhase.sequencing: Build bottom-up: schema first, then server routes, then kanban UI, then embed/comments, then federation. Each step leaves the plugin runnable — no half-built half-broken state.

tasks.title: Schema + migrations + basic server CRUD
tasks.details: Schema + server routes for boards and work items. No federation, no comments, no embeds, no client — those arrive in later tasks. Plugin-scoped table namespace plugin_pm_*.
tasks.resolution: packages/plugins/project-management/ with manifest.ts. Three tables created idempotently (CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS): plugin_pm_boards, plugin_pm_columns, plugin_pm_work_items. Server routes: POST /boards (creates with three seed columns: Todo / In Progress / Done), GET /boards?serverId=..., GET /boards/:slug (full detail with columns + work items grouped by column), POST /boards/:slug/work-items (positions at end of column), PATCH /work-items/:slug (edit + cross-column move with board-consistency check), DELETE /work-items/:slug. Sprints and comments deferred to later tasks. Plugin registered on server; client registration (setupClient) comes with task-pm-2. Three smoke tests on the manifest identity + migration shape. 256 tests pass.

tasks.title: Board list view — ListDetailView + empty kanban on select
tasks.details: Register a 'pm' view with ListDetailView: left rail = boards for the current server (search, create, select), right = selected board's columns as empty lanes. Sidebar slot button '📋 Boards' opens the view.
tasks.resolution: client.tsx + client-entry.ts shipped. Sidebar slot '📋 Boards' opens the PM view (server-scoped; hidden in DM mode). Main panel uses ListDetailView with boards on the left, BoardKanban on the right. Create-board flow uses the main panel for the form (same pattern polls adopted). Columns render side-by-side with empty-state messages; WorkItemCard component already exists but won't be populated until task-pm-3 wires item creation. All user strings wrapped in <T> — board names, descriptions, column names, card titles and descriptions translate automatically via the Phase 3 primitive.

tasks.title: Work item CRUD in the kanban UI
tasks.details: Create work item inline in a column (+ button → compact form). Click card to open detail. Edit in place. Delete.
tasks.resolution: Each column gets a '+ Add card' button that expands to an inline title input (rest of fields edited in the detail modal after). POST /work-items triggers a full board refetch via onMutated. Cards click into a read-only *sidebar preview* rendered by the 'task' embed (TaskPreview, all strings wrapped in <T>), and the preview's navigate handler fires a window EDIT_EVENT that BoardKanban listens for to pop WorkItemDetailModal — keeps the preview (read-only, translated) and the modal (edit surface) decoupled. Modal edits title / description / priority (low/medium/high/critical) / item type (task/bug/story/epic) plus Save and Delete. Assignee picker deferred to task-pm-5 — ships with comments so the picker UI has a real need. Translation-by-default: card title / description / column name all wrap <T>. The TaskPreview / TaskInline components also satisfy the 'task' half of task-pm-6, leaving only board refs + federation for that ticket.

tasks.title: Drag-and-drop reordering + cross-column moves
tasks.details: HTML5 drag-and-drop API (no deps). Drop on a column triggers PATCH /work-items/:slug with new column_id + position. Optimistic UI update with rollback on server error. Real-time sync via plugin:pm:updated WS broadcast so other viewers see the move. Handles the N+1 reorder problem with a position-rebalance on the server when positions get tight.
tasks.resolution: Cards are draggable via the HTML5 DnD API. Each card-slot has top/bottom halves that compute the insertion index; the empty-column and tail zones handle the first and last slots. Blue insertion-line CSS marks the target. Server exposes POST /boards/:slug/reorder { assignments: [{ columnId, itemIds: slug[] }] } — client sends the full new ordering for the 1-2 affected columns and the server rewrites positions sequentially (simpler + more robust than fractional-position math at this scale). Optimistic update applies the new order locally; on server error we roll back to the pre-drop snapshot. Refetch after success picks up canonical positions + any concurrent edits. Real-time sync across clients deferred to task-pm-7.

tasks.title: Work item comments thread
tasks.details: Each work item has a chatId (OrderedCollection) on creation — mirrors how events / files / wiki pages handle their comment threads. Use useChat(actor, chatId) in the detail view; get reactions + translations + threading for free. Comment content goes through the existing translation pipeline. Zero new chat code.
tasks.resolution: Work items create an OrderedCollection in the shared objects table at insert time (uri: /plugins/project-management/tasks/<slug>/chat, properties include isTaskChat + taskSlug for later federation discovery) and persist its id in plugin_pm_work_items.chat_id. WorkItemView exposes chatId to the client. WorkItemDetailModal renders a TaskComments pane when chatId is present — uses useChat(actor, chatId) with the existing MessageList + MessageInput + useTranslation pipeline, so translation / reactions / threading come for free. Work-item delete cleans up the OrderedCollection row. Assignee picker still deferred (sliding to pm-9 polish pass since comments shipped without needing it after all).

tasks.title: [[task:slug]] + [[board:slug]] embeds
tasks.details: Register two embed kinds. [[task:slug]] inline card: title + status badge + assignee avatar + priority indicator. Preview: full detail with comments. [[board:slug]] inline: board name + work-item count + top-3 columns. Preview: mini read-only kanban. Federation handlers resolveBySlug auto-wired via the plugin loader. Validates multiple-embed-kinds-per-plugin for the first time.
tasks.resolution: task kind landed with task-pm-3 (TaskInline + TaskPreview + Edit-via-EDIT_EVENT navigate). task-pm-6 adds the board kind: BoardInline (name + count badge) and BoardPreview (mini kanban with top-3 cards per column + overflow count). board navigate opens the PM view via EmbedNavCtx.openView('project-management') and defer-dispatches an OPEN_BOARD_EVENT that PmView listens for, selecting the target board. Server exposes federationHandlers.task and .board via resolveBySlug — the plugin loader auto-mounts /plugins/project-management/<kind>/by-slug/:slug so cross-tower [[server@tower:task:slug]] + [[server@tower:board:slug]] both resolve through the federation proxy. First plugin to ship two embed kinds + validates the federation handler surface is right.

tasks.title: Real-time sync across all PM mutations
tasks.details: plugin:pm:updated WS events on work item create/move/edit/delete and on comment create. Client-side cache invalidation + optimistic-update reconciliation. Follow the polls pattern (module-level onWsMessage subscription that publishes into a cache map).
tasks.resolution: Server broadcasts plugin:pm:updated { boardSlug, kind, slug? } on every mutation — board create, work-item create/patch/delete, reorder. Uses fastify.broadcastToAllSubscribers (same surface as polls). Client has two listeners: BoardKanban filters by boardSlug and refetches when a matching event arrives; PmView reloads the board list on any event (so counts and new boards stay fresh). Comment-message broadcasts are already handled by the core message pipeline since TaskComments uses useChat(actor, chatId) — no plugin-side work needed. Our own mutations still refetch via onMutated; hearing our own echo is at worst a redundant roundtrip, which keeps the code path singular.

tasks.title: Federation — AS2 custom objects for work items + boards
tasks.details: Work items federate as AS2 Note variants with type: 'WorkItem' + custom fields. Board metadata as Group properties. Inbox handler accepts remote Create/Update/Delete of work items from federated members. Cross-Tower [[server@tower:task:slug]] works through the proxy. Remote members can comment; comment federation reuses the existing Group-followers delivery pattern. This is the biggest chunk — validates AS2 custom object handling for the plugin system, not just message-shaped data.
tasks.resolution: Three layers shipped. (1) Plugin inbox dispatch hook: PluginInboxHandler interface in the SDK, registered per-objectType in the manifest's new inboxHandlers map. plugin-loader stores handlers; inbox.ts's handleCreate checks multi-typed objects (e.g. ['Note','WorkItem']), handleUpdate/handleDelete dispatch to plugin handlers before falling through to generic paths. (2) Outbound delivery: work-item create/update/delete wraps the item in a multi-typed AS2 ['Note','WorkItem'] activity with babelr:-namespaced fields (priority, itemType, boardId, columnId, slug) and delivers via Group-followers through the new fastify.deliverToGroupFollowers(groupId, activity) decoration — avoids importing server internals from plugin code, which would break the client Vite build. (3) Inbound handling: PM plugin registers a WorkItem inbox handler — handleCreate upserts into plugin_pm_work_items (ON CONFLICT slug DO UPDATE), handleUpdate patches fields, handleDelete removes the row + chat OrderedCollection. All three broadcast plugin:pm:updated WS so local clients sync in real-time. Board metadata doesn't federate as separate objects — boards are Group-scoped, remote towers fetch via the existing signed-proxy resolveBySlug.

tasks.title: Polish: translation coverage, edge cases, empty states
tasks.details: Sweep all user-facing strings through <T>. Validate translation works for work item titles + descriptions + comments end-to-end. Empty states for no-boards / no-work-items / no-comments. Error handling for deleted / moved items. Accessibility pass on drag-and-drop. Loading states for slow federation fetches. This is the sign-off pass before declaring PM done.
tasks.resolution: Translation sweep: all form labels (Name, Description, Title, Priority, Type), button labels (Save, Cancel, Delete, Add, Create board, Add card), status labels (Loading board/work item, Board/Task not found, Nothing here yet), heading text (New board, Work item, Boards, Comments), field labels (Points, Due, Created), and priority/type badge text now go through <T>. Board detail shows a 'Loading board…' placeholder while fetching (covers the selectedSlug && !selectedBoard case). DnD accessibility: cards have aria-roledescription='draggable card' + aria-label of the item title; columns have aria-label + aria-roledescription='kanban column'. Inline embeds wrap loading/error text in <T>. Error messages from server errors are structural and intentionally left un-translated.

deferred.title: Sprint planning + sprint view
deferred.reason: Useful but not required for validating the SDK. Ship core kanban first; sprints are additive once work item primitives exist.

deferred.title: Per-board permissions (viewers / editors / admins)
deferred.reason: Core PM inherits server-level CONNECT_VOICE / VIEW_CHANNELS style permissions for v1. Board-scoped roles are a polish item if there's demand.

deferred.title: Import from Jira / Linear / GitHub Issues
deferred.reason: The slack-teams-import roadmap item already covers this pattern for chat; extend when PM is stable. Not blocking the SDK validation goal.
